title: Phase 0 prerequisites
description: Land the shared v0.10.0 prerequisite work before the main component system: the `~/.openpalm/` home layout, validate-in-place rollback flow, vault-aware asset rewrites, and 38XX port standardization.

subtasks.description: Replace XDG path helpers with an OPENPALM_HOME-based home layout API in packages/lib and add legacy-layout detection for CLI and admin consumers.
Remove the permanent staging pipeline, add validate-in-place with rollback snapshots, and expose a rollback command path through lib and CLI.
Rewrite core compose, Caddy, env templates, and setup assets for the ~/.openpalm layout, vault boundary, and 38XX port standardization.

title: Unified component system
description: Deliver the 0.10.0 component model after the shared Phase 0 prerequisites land, with lib-owned lifecycle logic, migration tooling, and consumer updates across admin, CLI, registry, and docs.

subtasks.description: Add lib-owned component types, discovery, label parsing, overlay validation, and cross-component environment injection collision checks.

Implement enabled instance persistence and compose overlay assembly so `data/components/enabled.json` remains a system-managed runtime index, `config/openpalm.yml` stays the user-facing stack config, and vault plus instance env files are ordered correctly at deploy time.

Implement instance lifecycle operations in packages/lib, including instance directory creation, schema default seeding, secret-backed sensitive fields, Caddy snippet management, delete/archive cleanup, and start-stop compose rebuilds.
Replace channel-era admin APIs and DTOs with component and instance endpoints while keeping route handlers transport-only and delegating lifecycle logic to packages/lib.
Add CLI component commands and a full openpalm migrate flow that handles legacy XDG roots, env splitting, port updates, and channel-to-component conversion.
Replace the admin UI Containers and Registry views with a unified Components experience, schema-driven config forms, and migration guidance in setup and status flows.
Migrate the registry to registry/components, add CI validation for component directories and overlay safety, and update docs, fixtures, tests, and release readiness materials for the breaking change.

title: Password manager
description: Implement the 0.10.0 secrets backend, vault hardening, token split, plaintext and pass backends, admin secrets APIs, and migration-safe auth and secret handling.

subtasks.description: Replace legacy secrets.env and stack.env assumptions with vault-aware helpers, strict file-permission creation, two-file seeding, and generated redact schema inputs.

Extend shared control-plane state and startup flows to persist distinct admin and assistant credentials, including any needed `OPENPALM_ADMIN_TOKEN` compatibility handling and upgrade-time `ASSISTANT_TOKEN` backfill for existing installs.
Refactor admin auth helpers and route classification so admin-only operations keep requireAdmin while operational endpoints move to token-aware requireAuth semantics.

Add packages/lib secret backend contracts, backend detection, canonical secret mappings, and a default PlaintextBackend that routes writes between vault/user.env and vault/system.env without exposing values.
Implement the pass backend, provider config, validated pass entry naming, admin image dependencies, and pass-init and dev-setup script support for encrypted installs.
Add admin-only secrets API routes, metadata-only response shapes, and audit logging that records actor attribution while storing logs in the new logs/admin-audit.jsonl location.
Integrate component-sensitive field registration, provisioning, and cleanup into the shared secret backend so #301 lifecycle flows can manage secrets without duplicating backend logic.
Update compose mounts, startup ordering, scripts, docs, and automated coverage so the vault model, token split, secret APIs, backend behavior, and migration cases are verified end to end.

title: OpenViking integration
description: Add OpenViking as an optional 0.10.0 component with lib-owned config assembly, assistant tool wrappers, dual-mode memory plugin integration, and token-budget-aware context assembly.

subtasks.description: Create the registry/components/openviking directory with compose.yml and .env.schema, including a pinned Viking service, healthcheck, workspace and vault mounts, and additive assistant env injection only.
Add packages/lib control-plane helpers that validate Viking settings, assemble ov.conf as a whole file, and write it into the vault only through explicit control-plane flows.
Extend component validation and lifecycle coverage so the component system accepts Viking's assistant env injection, secret-backed schema fields, and vault config requirements.
Add a shared vikingFetch helper in assistant tools with API key injection, timeout and error normalization, and graceful disabled responses when Viking env vars are missing.
Implement the initial Viking tool surface for resource ingestion, search, read, browse, session-memory append, and session-memory discovery, with argument validation and stable output formatting.

Extend MemoryContextPlugin session-created and tool-executed hooks to create or reuse Viking sessions, retrieve cheap context in parallel with memory, and log tool outcomes without regressing memory-only behavior.
Add idle, delete, and compaction handling that commits Viking sessions safely, prefers summaries over full payloads, and tracks session-local Viking identifiers for idempotent correlation.
Add shared token-budget estimation and fit helpers plus a single assembleContext-style path that prefers Viking when present and falls back cleanly to memory-only retrieval.
Update operator docs and add focused lib and assistant-tools tests for ov.conf generation, Viking fetch and tool wrappers, token budgets, and dual-mode plugin behavior.

title: Admin OpenCode instance
description: Admin-authorized OpenCode instance inside the admin container, auto-started by entrypoint, accessed directly via localhost:3881. No broker, no intermediary — same pattern as assistant at localhost:3800.

subtasks.description: Add packages/lib helpers for admin OpenCode paths, runtime status types, session metadata DTOs, and dedicated seeded config locations that stay separate from normal assistant assets.
Replace the current background-process startup pattern with a supervisor or broker boundary that can start, stop, restart, and report health for the admin OpenCode child process deterministically.
Update the admin Dockerfile, launcher, and compose wiring for the 38XX port layout, local-only runtime binding, and runtime-only OpenCode and Bun dependencies without exposing a new public route.
Ship phase-1 status and start endpoints with admin auth, request IDs, idempotent semantics, and audit events for broker lifecycle operations.
Extend auth handling for elevated endpoints so admin-token requests and explicitly allowlisted assistant-token requests are distinguished correctly after the #300 token split.
Implement brokered elevated session creation and message proxy endpoints with strict payload and session ID validation, initiator metadata tagging, and admin-brokered downstream authorization.
Add structured audit events, caller and initiator attribution, sensitive-payload redaction, and broker failure classification for lifecycle and proxied session traffic.
Add an admin diagnostics UI touchpoint for runtime status and controlled elevated help entry without introducing a separate OpenCode UI route through Caddy.

Add lib, server, and integration coverage plus architecture docs updates to verify broker authz, proxy behavior, failure handling, port changes, and the absence of any new public elevated runtime route.

title: Azure Container Apps deployment
description: Add an Azure Container Apps deployment path with operator automation, Key Vault-backed secrets, Azure Files mounts, channel add flows, and supporting documentation without introducing a new cloud control plane.

subtasks.description: Add deploy/azure/deploy-aca.sh with setup, deploy, all, add-channel, status, and teardown subcommands and make resource creation idempotent where practical.

Check in a consistent ACA infrastructure definition set under deploy/azure that provisions the resource group, ACA environment, storage, file shares, Key Vault, managed identity, and per-service app settings.

Create Key Vault first, wire all operator-supplied and generated secrets through managed identity references, and keep per-app secret scope aligned with guardian, assistant, memory, and channel needs.
Map Azure Files shares for config, data, and work storage, seed the required directory layout during setup, and precreate the memory config file expected by the memory service.

Deploy memory, assistant, guardian, and channel-chat as separate ACA apps in dependency order with correct ingress modes, internal FQDN wiring, probes, scaling, and public exposure limited to the channel edge.
Implement the add-channel deployment flow so a new channel app can be provisioned with its own Key Vault secret, guardian wiring, mounts, probes, and optional external ingress.

Add deploy/azure/README.md documenting prerequisites, roles, topology, Key Vault and ingress behavior, operational differences from self-hosted mode, troubleshooting, and a regression smoke-test checklist.
Update the root README and changelog to position ACA as an additive deployment target with no Admin UI or self-hosted control plane parity.

Validate the deployment automation with bash syntax checks, shellcheck when available, and a real Azure smoke test that covers deploy, request flow, persistence, add-channel, teardown, and secret exposure checks.
